name: Back up the database with Notion and Google Drive automatically

parameters.message: =🛑 Tonight's backup came out EMPTY — investigate immediately.

parameters.text: =✅ Back up the database with Notion and Google Drive automatically — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 4 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Export All Rows (Notion)

### Export All Rows (Notion)

notion — `getAll` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Serialize to CSV · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Serialize to CSV

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Compress Archive

### Compress Archive

Zips the binary to cut storage/transfer size before upload.

Next: Upload Backup to Google Drive

### Upload Backup to Google Drive

googleDrive — `upload` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Backup Size Sane? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Upload Backup to Google Drive

parameters.content:
### Backup Size Sane?

Two-way gate on `={{ ($binary.data?.fileSize ?? 1) > 0 }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Align Field Names · ②→ Alarm — Empty Backup

### Alarm — Empty Backup

slack — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Sort Payloads Newest First

### Sort Payloads Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

### Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
# Back up the database with Notion and Google Drive automatically

**Pattern:** Backup & Archive · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Back up the database with Notion and Google Drive automatically. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual webhooks is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Export All Rows (Notion) → Serialize to CSV → Compress Archive → Upload Backup to Google Drive → Backup Size Sane? → Align Field Names → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to backup & archive without manual effort
- Anyone automating webhooks in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2; Microsoft OAuth2; Notion integration token; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your webhooks process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
